title: Legal/compliance memo — signals as financial advice
acceptance_criteria: User drafts factual description in docs/compliance/signals-memo-facts.md: what the product is, exactly (AI-generated signals, 13-agent ensemble + Bittensor consensus, tier-gated latency, no order execution on behalf of customers, no custody of customer funds, no personalization beyond tier)
User engages a securities-aware attorney (referral from r/algotrading, r/legaladvice, or existing counsel). Budget: $500-2000 for a 1-2hr review

Lawyer returns a one-page memo addressing: (a) is this investment advice requiring SEC / state adviser registration under our fact pattern, (b) required disclaimers and language, (c) any prohibited claims we must not make in marketing, (d) any state-level carve-outs (e.g., do we avoid CA residents, NY residents, etc.)
Memo filed at docs/compliance/signals-memo.md (redacted for public if needed; full version in private store)
Derived actions added to Track C (e.g., update C.05 to inject disclaimer in responses, update C.10 to show disclaimer on pricing page)
Memo has a validity date; schedule a re-review annually or on product-surface changes
deliverables: trading/api/routes/signals.py — disclaimer injection (if required by memo)
frontend/src/pages/pricing — disclaimer text (if required by memo)
user_driven_note: Agent can help draft the factual description (signals-memo-facts.md) but MUST NOT write the legal conclusion. Only a licensed attorney should produce the signals-memo.md content. No fake legal guidance.
